billing.plan-desc: * Upgrading your plan will take effect immediately. You'll be charged the prorated amount for the remainder of your current billing cycle.

success.active-plan: Your active plan will be updated immediately.

success.billing-cycle-desc: Your billing cycle has started. You will be charged on the same date each month.

billing.switch-to-seat-based-description: Pay per workspace member instead of a fixed monthly fee.

app_category_descriptions.ai: AI apps for assistants, simulations, and creative thinking.
app_category_descriptions.all: Every routable Tuturuuu app available from this launcher.
app_category_descriptions.content: Content apps for publishing, files, and delivery.
app_category_descriptions.core: Core entrypoints for the Tuturuuu workspace platform.
app_category_descriptions.developer: Developer utilities, gateways, and technical tools.
app_category_descriptions.learning: Learning apps for courses, practice, and teaching.
app_category_descriptions.miscellaneous: Miscellaneous apps for docs, utilities, and public shortcuts.
app_category_descriptions.operations: Operations apps for money, inventory, and commerce.
app_category_descriptions.productivity: Productivity apps for schedule, tasks, messages, and meetings.

app_descriptions.apps: Browse and launch every app available in your workspace.
app_descriptions.calendar: Plan schedules, events, and shared availability.
app_descriptions.chat: Message teammates and keep workspace conversations together.
app_descriptions.cms: Create and publish structured content for sites and products.
app_descriptions.contacts: Organize customers, members, and relationship history.
app_descriptions.docs: Read product guides, references, and setup instructions.
app_descriptions.drive: Store, organize, and share workspace files.
app_descriptions.finance: Manage wallets, transactions, invoices, and budgets.
app_descriptions.hive: Explore collaborative simulations and spatial experiences.
app_descriptions.inventory: Manage products, stock, purchasing, and sales.
app_descriptions.learn: Follow courses, assignments, and learning progress.
app_descriptions.mail: Read and send workspace email from one inbox.
app_descriptions.meet: Schedule meetings and find a time that works for everyone.
app_descriptions.mind: Build mind maps and connect ideas with AI.
app_descriptions.nova: Create and solve prompt-driven AI challenges.
app_descriptions.pay: Manage billing, subscriptions, and payment activity.
app_descriptions.platform: Manage your workspace, settings, and the full Tuturuuu platform.
app_descriptions.rewise: Practice, review, and retain knowledge with AI.
app_descriptions.shortener: Create compact links and manage redirects.
app_descriptions.storefront: Sell products through a customer-facing online store.
app_descriptions.tasks: Plan projects, assign work, and track progress.
app_descriptions.teach: Plan courses, manage classes, and support learners.
app_descriptions.tools: Open focused utilities for QR codes and quick tasks.
app_descriptions.track: Record time and understand where work goes.

preferences.keyboard_shortcuts_description: Review keyboard shortcuts for app and sidebar actions

teach.familyBody: Publish handoff notes that make sense beside Learn progress, parent views, and the central workspace record.
teach.footer: Teach stays light: plan in context, manage in Tuturuuu, and send learners to Learn.
teach.handoffBody: Open learner previews with the same education workspace context instead of guessing what students will see.
teach.heroLead: Teach is the teacher-facing app for course user groups, classroom signals, and the daily loop between modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.
teach.heroPanelBody: Public landing stays here. After sign-in, teachers enter a workspace dashboard backed by the central Tuturuuu account.
teach.planBody: Sketch the next class loop, then jump into apps/web for the real workspace objects.
teach.signalBody: Keep attention on what needs teaching action, not another dashboard copy.
teach.studioBody: Course user groups, modules, attendance, reports, and metrics stay framed as one teaching workspace.
teach.syncBody: Teach forwards protected API traffic to apps/web so workspace ownership stays centralized.

teachDashboard.emptyGroupsBody: Create workspace user groups in apps/web first. Teach will present each group as a course group with modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.

attendance.body: Open the centralized attendance surface for the selected workspace groups.
attendance.title: Attendance pulse

groups.body: Use workspace user groups as course cohorts with a clear teaching owner.
groups.title: Course groups

metrics.body: Review score metrics before they become learner and parent-facing progress.
metrics.title: Metrics lab

modules.body: Keep lesson sequence, module coverage, and next steps tied to the group.
modules.title: Module map

preview.body: Open the learner app to inspect how the course path appears outside Teach.
preview.title: Learner preview

reports.body: Prepare reporting loops from the same groups, attendance, and score metrics.
reports.title: Report studio

featureGrid.lead: Six high-frequency teaching paths stay visible so Teach feels like a classroom operations dashboard, not a landing page.
featureGrid.title: Course group command center

teachDashboard.groupsLead: In apps/web these are workspace user groups. In Teach, treat each one as a course group with learners, modules, attendance, reports, and metrics.
teachDashboard.lead: Manage the teaching loop without turning Teach into a second platform admin. The dashboard focuses on groups, modules, centralized attendance, reports, and score metrics.

attendance.body: Reconcile attendance with the current course group before class context goes stale.
attendance.title: Attendance queue

metrics.body: Review the score metrics that will shape learner progress and parent-facing reports.
metrics.title: Metrics review

plan.body: Check module coverage and prepare the next lesson sequence for each active course group.
plan.title: Module planning

reports.body: Prepare report notes while the group status, attendance, and scores are still nearby.
reports.title: Report prep

teachDashboard.operationsLead: A focused board for the next teaching actions that usually happen after opening course groups.
teachDashboard.realtimeLead: Top 5 active courses — students, pending grading, unsubmitted work, and low scorers at a glance.
teachDashboard.statsUnavailableBody: Try again shortly to see pending grading, missing submissions, and low-scorer alerts.
teachDashboard.title: Run today’s teaching dashboard.

attendance.body: Use centralized attendance for the group, then keep the result tied to the same workspace record.
attendance.title: Check attendance once

groups.body: Start with workspace user groups and read them as dedicated course groups inside Teach.
groups.title: Own the course group

metrics.body: Scores in the classroom map to platform metrics, so reporting stays consistent across Teach, Learn, and apps/web.
metrics.title: Review score metrics

modules.body: Attach the lesson sequence to the group so students see the right module path in Learn.
modules.title: Sequence modules

context.hint: Optional. Add the class level, learning goals, or any constraints the AI should follow.
context.label: Teacher context
context.placeholder: For example: Focus on beginner-friendly explanations and include review questions after each module.

teachOperations.noReportsYetBody: Create a learner report and it will appear here for preview and follow-up.

teachOperations.previewEmptyFeedback: Learner feedback preview will appear here.
teachOperations.reportComposerLead: Draft once, preview exactly what the learner-facing report will communicate, then save it to the course.

reports.lead: Write course reports for enrolled learners from inside Teach.
reports.title: Reports

teachOperations.scheduleLead: Set the class term and recurring weekdays. Teach seeds these as 7:00-8:00 AM GMT+7 sessions for attendance.
